ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing A Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-01-2023 Continued To Apr-12-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ronald Chun appeared by zoom for and with Evelyn Fong by zoom.

Mr. Chun apologized for the non-appearance at the last hearing, stated that there was a medical emergency which required going out of State, that the petition for final distribution and the report are on his computer, that he will be working on it this week, and that he will get it signed next week. Mr. Chun also stated that they got the income taxes done. Status on close of estate is continued to April 12, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If Mr. Chun is unable to file a petition for final distribution by then, he is to file a petition for approval of report on status of administration. The order to appear is off calendar.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Executor and her Counsel to appear on March 1, 2023, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration has not been filed. Today's status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Choi. Matter not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Closing Estate
Off Calendar Feb-09-2022 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-09-2022 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Closing Estate
Off Calendar Dec-01-2021 Continued To Feb-09-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ronald Chun, via ZOOM, for Evelyn Fong, via ZOOM, Petitioner.

Mr. Chun reports that the Inventory and Appraisal ("INVAP") was sent to the probate referee on Nov-24-2021 and that he submitted a report on status of administration for e-filing, which is confirmed by the court. The court also confirms payment of sanctions. The court reminds Mr. Chun that he failed to comply with previous court orders. The court advises that it will be granting a continuance of this matter and instructs Mr. Chun to file the INVAP seven days prior to the next calling of this matter and that if for any reason the report on status of administration that is pending in the court's e-filing queue is rejected, that too will need to be filed seven days prior to the next calling of this matter. The court advises Mr. Chun that if there are issues with filing any of the previously mentioned documents, Mr. Chun shall submit to the court good cause, via a declaration, as to why the documents have not been filed. Mr. Chun is advised that if he does not follow the court's directives regarding the previously mentioned filing deadlines, sanctions will begin seven days prior to the next calling of this matter until the documents have been filed.

The Status of Filing of Inventory and Appraisal and Petition for Final Distribution or Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration, and Order to Show Cause are continued to Feb-09-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-01-2021 Continued To Feb-09-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Closing Estate
Off Calendar Nov-03-2021 Continued To Dec-01-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ronald Chun, via ZOOM, for Evelyn Fong, Personal Representative.

Counsel reports that his client could not attend today and assures the court that papers will be filed soon and that he has been dealing with health issues. Counsel reassures the court that he will work diligently and that he has retained outside counsel to assist him with this matter.

The court advises that it is imposing sanctions of $250 on Mr. Chun and sanctions of $250 on Personal Representative Evelyn Fong. Sanctions are to be paid to the Clerk of the Court within ten (10) days of this hearing and receipts regarding payment are to be submitted to the court. Sanctions of $50 per day are imposed on both Ronald Chun and Evelyn Fong as of Nov-04-2021, until an Inventory and Appraisal AND a Petition are formally filed with the court. An Order to Show Cause as to why the Personal Representative should not be suspended or as to why the court should not require a bond in the amount of $350,000 is set for Dec-01-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204. A Status of Filing Inventory and Appraisal is set for Dec-01-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204. If an Inventory and Appraisal is not filed one week prior to Dec-01-2021, the court will consider suspending the Personal Representative.

The Status on Close of Estate is continued to Dec-01-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Closing Estate
Off Calendar Aug-25-2021 Continued To Nov-03-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ronald Chun appeared by zoom for and with Evelyn Fong by zoom.

Mr. Chun stated that this has been a difficult case for him to finish, that his client understands his situation, that his client has agreed to bring in other Counsel, that they are bringing in Michael Spalding, and requested a 60-day continuance. Mr. Chun also stated that the funds are all protected, that no distributions have been made to any heirs, that the only disbursement was for the taxes and that all of the real property is being handled through the trust. In addition, Mr. Chun stated that ownership interests need to be determined for the laundry mat as well as the restaurant, and that the intent is to file an inventory and appraisal, and a status report. Status on close of estate is continued to November 3, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Mr. Chun that if said documents are not filed by October 27, 2021, sanctions will begun to run in an amount of $50.00 per day against Mr. Chun and Ms. Fong.

Judge: Vedica Puri. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Aug-25-2021 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Closing Estate
Off Calendar Jul-28-2021 Continued To Aug-25-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court noted that this matter was previously on calendar on June 30, 2021, for status on filing of the inventory and appraisal, and for filing of a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration. Counsel was informed at that hearing that the referenced documents had to be filed by the next calling of the matter or the court would consider imposing sanctions suspending the Personal Representative. An Order to Show Cause will be issued to the Executor Evelyn D. Fong and the Attorney Ronald Chun to appear on August 25, 2021, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear, why the inventory and appraisal, and a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration have not been filed, and to show cause as to why sanctions in the amount of $50.00 per day as of today until the date of filing of said documents against each the Executor and Counsel should not be imposed, and why Ms. Fong should not be removed as the Executor of the estate. Status on close of estate is also continued to that date.

Judge: Hon. Mary Wiss. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: B. Tso. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Closing Estate
Off Calendar Jul-26-2021 Continued To Jul-28-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Closing Estate
Off Calendar Jun-30-2021 Continued To Jul-26-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court noted that this matter is on calendar for status on filing of the inventory and appraisal, and for filing of a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration, that it was on calendar on April 7, 2021 where Counsel was informed that he needed to file said documents by May 19, 2021 and that nothing was filed. The Court also noted that Counsel appeared at the hearing on June 2, 2021 and was informed at that time that if nothing was filed at the next calling, the Court would impose sanctions and suspend the Personal Representative. An Order to Appear as well as an Order to Show Cause will be issued to the Executor Evelyn D. Fong and the Attorney Ronald Chun to appear on July 26, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear, why the inventory and appraisal, and a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration have not been filed, and to show cause as to why sanctions in the amount of $50.00 per day as of today until the date of filing of said documents against each the Executor and Counsel should not be imposed, and why Ms. Fong should not be removed as the Executor of the estate. Status on close of estate is also continued to that date.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Johnson. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Closing Estate
Off Calendar Jun-02-2021 Continued To Jun-30-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ronald Chun, via ZOOM, counsel for Evelyn Fong, Executor.

At the outset, the court reminds counsel that it has been more than two years that this matter has been with the court and to date the court has not received an Inventory and Appraisal nor a Petition for Final Distribution or a Petition for Approval of Status Report. Counsel reports that the only asset in this estate is a real property located outside of California and that once the ancillary probate in that jurisdiction is resolved he intends to submit an Inventory and Appraisal. Counsel also reports that he has yet to speak to the probate referee assigned to this matter, Lawrence Lee. The court points to the fact that Lawrence Lee has retired, and Karen Ann Simons is now the probate referee assigned to this matter, which demonstrates counsel's failure in managing this matter.

After additional discussion, the court advises counsel that unless, at the very least, a partial Inventory and Appraisal and either a Petition for Final Distribution or a Petition for Approval of Status Report have not been filed by the next calling of this matter, the court intends to impose sanctions and suspend the Administrator.

The Status of Closing of Estate is continued to June 30, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Counsel is ordered to file the documents referenced above by June 21, 2021.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabaseres Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Closing Estate
Off Calendar Apr-07-2021 Continued To Jun-02-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ronald Chun, via ZOOM, counsel for Evelyn Fong, Executor.

At the outset, counsel apologizes for the delay in closing the estate and requests a ninety (90) day continuance. He also advises of partial interests in both a restaurant and a grocery store and he hopes to resolve those issues soon. The court advises that the delay in closing the estate in unacceptable and the court typically expects most cases to be closed within 18 months after letters are first issued. The court advises Mr. Chun that a petition for final distribution or a petition for the approval of report on status of administration and an Inventory and Appraisal and an accounting or complete waivers are to be submitted two weeks prior, MAY-19-2021, to the next hearing on JUN-02-2021. The court also advises Mr. Chun that if the filing deadlines are not met, the court will contemplate sanctions.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: C. Lee. Not reported.
